title: Replace photo frame overlay with premium border and add WhatsApp sharing button

requirements.text: In FloatingPhotoFrame.tsx and BaseTemplate.tsx, remove the decorative filigree frame/overlay SVG element that renders on top of or in the middle of the bride and groom photos. Replace it with a premium CSS border/boundary styled directly on the photo container — use a multi-layered border approach (e.g., a thick solid or double gold/saffron border with a subtle inset shadow and an outer glow) that frames the photo from the outside without any element overlapping the photo image itself. The photo must be fully visible at all times. The border style should feel premium and on-theme (gold tones, rounded or slightly ornate corners using border-radius or a clip-path, consistent with the Hindu wedding aesthetic). The floating 3D bobbing animation on the photo container should be retained.

quotes: REMOVE THE FRAME FROM BRIDE AND GROOM IT SHOWS IN MIDDLE OR OVERLAP ON PHOTO INSTEAD ADD A BORDER OR BOUNDARY AROUND PHOTO WHICH MAKES IT PREMIUM

acceptanceCriteria: No SVG filigree or overlay element renders on top of or across the bride/groom photo area.
The photo is fully visible without any element covering any portion of the image.
A premium-looking border/boundary is visible around the outside edge of each photo container.
The border uses gold/saffron tones consistent with the app's Hindu wedding theme.
The border styling looks premium across all 8 templates in both light and dark variants.
The floating 3D bobbing animation on the photo frame container is still present and functional.
The reduce-motion toggle still disables the bobbing animation when enabled.

requirements.text: Add a WhatsApp sharing button to the invitation page (InvitePage.tsx or ShareSection.tsx). The button should be visible on the published invitation page at /invite/:id. When clicked, it opens a WhatsApp share link in a new tab using the format https://wa.me/?text=<encoded message> where the message includes the couple names, wedding date, and the full shareable invitation URL. Style the button to match the app's premium theme (gold/saffron accent, Cormorant Garamond or Cinzel typography). Place it near the existing share/QR section or as a floating button.

quotes: IT NEED AN WHATSAPP SHARING BUTTON ALSO

acceptanceCriteria: A WhatsApp sharing button is visible on every published invitation page at /invite/:id.
Clicking the button opens a new browser tab with a wa.me share URL.
The WhatsApp share message includes the couple names, wedding date, and the full invitation URL.
The button is styled consistently with the app's premium Hindu wedding theme (gold accents, matching typography).
The button is responsive and visible on both mobile and desktop viewports.
The WhatsApp icon or label clearly identifies the button as a WhatsApp share action.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ (immutable paths).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/components/ui (immutable paths).
The floating 3D bobbing animation on photo frames must be preserved; only the overlapping frame overlay element is to be removed.
WhatsApp sharing must use the standard wa.me URL scheme — no third-party SDKs or APIs.
nonGoals: Adding any other social sharing platforms beyond WhatsApp.
Changing the backend schema or adding new backend endpoints.
Modifying the overall layout or structure of the invitation templates beyond the photo frame and share section changes.
Changing how photos are uploaded or stored.
